description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the condaforge/linux-anvil-cos7-x86_64:latest Docker image, define stages lint, build, test, post-test, and deploy, and set the PACKAGE_VERSION variable to 0.2.9. In the lint stage, the lint job (with PYTHON_VERSION=3.10) runs the .configure before_script to write a conda config and source conda, then executes: cd $(dirname ${CI_CONFIG_PATH}); conda install -yq "python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" isort flake8 black mypy; python -m isort ${CI_PROJECT_NAME} --check --diff; python -m flake8; python -m black --config pyproject.toml --check .; python -m mypy -p ${CI_PROJECT_NAME} || true. In the build stage, the build job runs in parallel for PYTHON_VERSION 3.10 and 3.9, runs: conda install -yq conda conda-build conda-verify conda-forge-pinning; cd "${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/.ci/conda"; conda build . --variant-config-files /opt/conda/conda_build_config.yaml --variants "{python: [${PYTHON_VERSION}], numpy: [1.16], python_impl: [cpython]}" --no-test --output-folder "$CI_PROJECT_DIR/conda-bld" and stores the conda-bld directory as an artifact. In the test stage, the test job runs in a matrix of PYTHON_VERSION (3.10,3.9) and SUBPKG_NAME (db_tools,df_tools,ml_tools,py_tools,sequence_tools,structure_tools,system_tools), executes: find ${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/conda-bld; conda index ${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/conda-bld; ls -al ${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/conda-bld/linux-64; conda create -n test -y -q -c "file://${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/conda-bld" "python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" ${CI_PROJECT_NAME} weblogo pillow muscle pytest pytest-cov hypothesis; conda activate test; PKG_INSTALL_DIR=$(python -c "import kmtools.${SUBPKG_NAME}; print(kmtools.${SUBPKG_NAME}.__path__[0])"); rm -rf kmtools; python -m pytest -c setup.cfg --cov="${PKG_INSTALL_DIR}" --cov-config=setup.cfg --color=yes "tests/${SUBPKG_NAME}/"; mkdir coverage; mv .coverage coverage/.coverage.${SUBPKG_NAME} and always uploads the coverage files and conda-bld as artifacts. In the post-test stage, the docs job (PYTHON_VERSION=3.10) runs: conda create -n test -y -q -c "file://${CI_PROJECT_DIR}/conda-bld" "python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" "${CI_PROJECT_NAME}" nbconvert ipython ipykernel pandoc; conda activate test; pip install -q sphinx sphinx_rtd_theme recommonmark nbsphinx coverage; (cd docs && sphinx-apidoc ../$CI_PROJECT_NAME -o api/generated -TeMP); sphinx-build docs public; coverage combine coverage/; coverage report || true; coverage html || true; mv htmlcov public/ || true and publishes the public directory as an artifact. In the deploy stage, the deploy job installs twine, renames any *-linux_x86_64.whl to manylinux1_x86_64.whl, and conditionally uploads built packages: if PACKAGE_VERSION contains "dev" it uploads tarballs to Anaconda with a dev label; if a git tag exists it uploads tarballs to Anaconda and wheels to PyPI via twine. Additionally, a pages job runs only on master and tags (excluding triggers), installs unzip via yum, moves the generated public folder to a versioned directory, creates a fresh public directory, then for each git tag downloads the docs artifact from that tag, extracts it into public, and publishes the public directory as a pages artifact.
